title: raukaua simplex
intro: Raukaua simplex, also known as New Zealand native celery or Kawakawa relative, is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Araliaceae. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils. The health benefits of Raukaua simplex include relief from fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Raukaua simplex can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional Maori medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
